ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301103 covers green tea beverage as a category; no specific code exists for the Ayataka brand product. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers tea and tea-based beverages; no brand-specific code for Ayataka. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 refers to green tea as a substance; used as closest parent for this RTD green tea product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 500 ml bottle of Ayataka green tea, as typically sold and consumed as a single-serve ready-to-drink beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Ayataka Green Tea — Nutritional Information (Coca-Cola Japan product label data cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Green Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water, NDB No. 14355)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central; Coca-Cola Japan product specification
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Ayataka is a Coca-Cola Japan RTD green tea. The product is essentially brewed green tea with no added sugar in its standard formulation. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917), which is the closest approved-source match. The product label confirms 0 kcal per 100 ml, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate, and approximately 1 mg sodium per 100 ml, consistent with USDA brewed green tea data. Values are reported per 100 g (equivalent to 100 ml for this near-water-density beverage).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages: plain brewed green tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates; GI conventionally assigned as effectively zero
gi_source.source_match_notes: Ayataka unsweetened green tea contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 ml. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature confirm that plain brewed green tea and other zero-carbohydrate beverages carry no glycemic load. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) per Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages; gi_category is low; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (~1 mg per 100 ml); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Ayataka unsweetened green tea contains approximately 1 mg sodium per 100 ml, making it an appropriate beverage choice for individuals managing hypertension; epidemiological evidence also suggests regular green tea consumption may modestly support blood pressure reduction via catechin-mediated vasodilation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ayataka unsweetened green tea contains 0 g carbohydrate and 0 g sugar per 100 ml, producing no postprandial glucose response; it is a suitable calorie-free beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, and green tea catechins (EGCG) have been associated with improved insulin sensitivity in clinical studies.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat; zero cholesterol; green tea catechins with cardioprotective associations
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ayataka unsweetened green tea contains no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and no added sugar, and regular consumption of green tea has been associated in meta-analyses with modest reductions in LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular event risk, making it a heart-friendly beverage choice.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayataka (綾鷹) is a premium ready-to-drink (RTD) Japanese green tea produced by Coca-Cola Japan under the Authentic Tea House brand. It is brewed from high-quality Camellia sinensis leaves using a traditional Japanese tea-brewing method that emphasises a mild, slightly astringent flavour with a clean, refreshing finish characteristic of high-grade Japanese green tea. The beverage contains no added sugar, no artificial sweeteners, no preservatives, and no calories, making it nutritionally equivalent to freshly brewed unsweetened green tea. Per 100 ml, it provides approximately 0 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, and 0 g carbohydrate, with trace minerals including potassium (~8 mg), sodium (~1 mg), and magnesium (~1 mg). The product is widely sold across Asia, including Singapore, where it is available through food delivery platforms and convenience stores. Clinically, Ayataka is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (zero glycemic load), hypertension (negligible sodium), and cardiovascular disease (no saturated fat or cholesterol). Green tea catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), present in the brewed tea, have been associated with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective effects in peer-reviewed literature. The beverage is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and halal-suitable.
common_user_queries: How many calories does Ayataka have?
What is the GI of Ayataka green tea?
Where is Ayataka green tea from?

confidence_reason: Ayataka is a well-documented commercial RTD green tea product by Coca-Cola Japan. Its nutritional profile (zero calories, zero macronutrients, trace minerals) is confirmed by the product label and is consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917). The zero-macro values are inherent to unsweetened brewed green tea and are confirmed zero by the approved source.
notes: Ayataka is an unsweetened ready-to-drink green tea; energy_kcal is naturally zero as this beverage contains no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero as brewed green tea contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as brewed green tea contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this is an unsweetened tea with no added sugars or starch. sugar_g is confirmed zero per product formulation and USDA FoodData Central brewed green tea data. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as the tea leaves are not consumed. Vitamin B1, B2, B3, B6, folate, vitamin E, and selenium are -1 as these are not reported in the USDA FoodData Central brewed green tea entry (FDC ID 171917) at meaningful levels and are not reported on the product label. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. The food_name has been normalised from the field survey menu label 'Authentic Tea House®™ Ayataka' to the canonical product name 'Ayataka Green Tea'. The cuisine is classified as Japanese given the product's origin. Multilingual names are provided for Japanese (mandatory), and additionally for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to support Singapore market retrieval. The field survey source restaurant is Khao - Funan, Singapore; price SGD 4.00.

improvement_actions_taken: Normalised food_name from field survey menu label 'Authentic Tea House®™ Ayataka' to canonical product name 'Ayataka Green Tea'.
Classified cuisine as Japanese (product origin) rather than inheriting 'Healthy' cuisine tag from field survey.
Added mandatory Japanese local name 綾鷹 and additional multilingual names for Singapore market (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Set all zero core macros with explicit zero-justification phrases in notes per zero-macro justification rule.
Set glycemic_index=1 per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule; documented in notes and gi_source.
Set -1 sentinel values for unreported micronutrients with explicit documentation in notes per sentinel values documentation rule.
Used only approved sources: USDA FoodData Central (secondary global source) and University of Sydney GI Database.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent.
